title: trichosanthes villosa subsp. mindorensis
intro: Trichosanthes villosa subsp. mindorensis, also known as the Mindoro cucumber or snake gourd, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cucurbitaceae. It is used in traditional medicine in the Philippines for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Trichosanthes villosa subsp. mindorensis include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as tea, infusion, or dried extract, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
